TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan katua qaḍis (Kuɫaɫau village, p.228)

S.TRANSL: There was Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan Vavuluvulungan.

S.FORM: izua ti Tjukutjuku a Kaḍangiangian i qinaɫan a mamaw.
S.PHON: izua ti cukucuku a kaɖaŋiaŋian i qinaʎan a mamaw.
S.FORM: izua ti Tjukutjuku a Kadrangiangian i qinaljan a mamaw.
S.PHON: izua ti ʦukuʦuku a kaɖaŋiaŋian i qinaʎan a mamaw.
S.TRANSL: There was Tjukutjuku Kaḍangiangian in the same village.

S.FORM: izua kuku ni Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan a qaḍis.
S.PHON: izua kuku ni puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a qaɖis.
S.FORM: izua kuku ni Puljaljuljaljuyan a qadris.
S.PHON: izua kuku ni puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a qaɖis.
S.TRANSL: Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan had a pet hawk.

S.FORM: nu kisudju ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan a sema Kaḍangiangian, avanu qaqivuin azua qaḍis.
S.PHON: nu kisuɉu ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a səma kaɖaŋiaŋian, avanu qaqivuin azua qaɖis.
S.FORM: nu kisudju ti Puljaljuljaljuyan a sema Kadrangiangian, avanu qaqivuin azua qadris.
S.PHON: nu kisuɉu ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a səma kaɖaŋiaŋian, avanu qaqivuin azua qaɖis.
S.TRANSL: When Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan went courting to the Kaḍangiangian house, he called the hawk to go along.
On the way there was a field at Djaɫalu.

S.FORM: manu vaik tia Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan katua qaḍis.
S.PHON: manu vaik tia puʎaʎuʎaʎujan katua qaɖis.
S.FORM: manu vaik tia Puljaljuljaljuyan katua qadris.
S.PHON: manu vaik tia puʎaʎuʎaʎujan katua qaɖis.
S.TRANSL: Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan and the hawk went off.

S.FORM: manu rinaung nua qaɫa a se Ɫinasalas.
S.PHON: manu rinauŋ nua qaʎa a sə ʎinasaɭas.
S.FORM: manu rinaung nua qalja a se Ljinasalas.
S.PHON: manu rinauŋ nua qaʎa a sə ʎinasaɭas.
S.TRANSL: Enemies from Ɫinasalas lay in ambush.

S.FORM: manu kemɫang a qaḍis tu izua qaɫa.
S.PHON: manu kəmʎaŋ a qaɖis tu izua qaʎa.
S.FORM: manu kemljang a qadris tu izua qalja.
S.PHON: manu kəmʎaŋ a qaɖis tu izua qaʎa.
S.TRANSL: The hawk knew there were enemies there.
“Come on, let’s go back, Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan; there are enemies here.”
“Never mind,” said Puɫaɫaɫuɫuyan.

S.FORM: manu djemaɫun tua quma, sa kuangi nua qaɫa ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan; manu matsay.
S.PHON: manu ɉəmaʎun tua quma, sa kuaŋi nua qaʎa ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan; manu maʦaj.
S.FORM: manu djemaljun tua quma, sa kuangi nua qalja ti Puljaljuljaljuyan; manu macay.
S.PHON: manu ɉəmaʎun tua quma, sa kuaŋi nua qaʎa ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan; manu maʦaj.
S.TRANSL: They reached the field, and Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan was shot by the enemies, and died.

S.FORM: "'ai anga ti kaka i Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan," aya a kuku a qaḍis.
S.PHON: "'ai aŋa ti kaka i puʎaʎuʎaʎujan," aja a kuku a qaɖis.
S.FORM: "'ai anga ti kaka i Puljaljuljaljuyan," aya a kuku a qadris.
S.PHON: "ʔai aŋa ti kaka i puʎaʎuʎaʎujan," aja a kuku a qaɖis.
S.TRANSL: “Oh poor brother Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan!” said the pet hawk.
“What can I do to bring my brother back to life?”
Well he came back to life.
People came to the rescue.
“What’s all the fuss about?” said Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
“We thought you were dead.”
“Dead indeed, a tough male like me!
Come on, let’s just go home.”
When he got home,
his mother was crying.
“Don’t cry, mother, I’m still alive.
It’s nothing to a male like me.”
That’s the end of the story.
